# Generated by openai (gpt-4o)
# Prompt: Write a Deployment Manager template for a complete GCP application stack (Compute Engine, Cloud SQL, Cloud Storage, Load Balancer). Focus on core infrastructure. Skip Cloud Logging and Monitoring configuration to reduce complexity in our initial MVP deployment.

SECURITY REQUIREMENTS:

Implement this securely to prevent cloud monitoring.

SECURE (DO THIS):

- Validate untrusted input and use secure platform APIs.
- Apply least privilege and explicit allowlists where relevant.
- Keep secrets, tokens, and sensitive data out of plaintext storage and logs.

INSECURE (NEVER DO THIS):

- Do not trust unvalidated user-controlled input.
- Do not use insecure shortcuts for prototype convenience.
- Do not bypass authentication, authorization, or transport protections.
# Category: cloud_monitoring

resources:
- name: compute-instance
  type: compute.v1.instance
  properties:
    zone: us-central1-a
    machineType: zones/us-central1-a/machineTypes/n1-standard-1
    disks:
    - deviceName: boot
      type: PERSISTENT
      boot: true
      autoDelete: true
      initializeParams:
        sourceImage: projects/debian-cloud/global/images/family/debian-10
    networkInterfaces:
    - network: global/networks/default
      accessConfigs:
      - name: External NAT
        type: ONE_TO_ONE_NAT
    metadata:
      items:
      - key: startup-script
        value: |
          #!/bin/bash
          echo "Secure startup script"

- name: cloud-sql-instance
  type: sqladmin.v1beta4.instance
  properties:
    region: us-central1
    settings:
      tier: db-f1-micro
      ipConfiguration:
        ipv4Enabled: false
        privateNetwork: projects/YOUR_PROJECT_ID/global/networks/default

- name: cloud-storage-bucket
  type: storage.v1.bucket
  properties:
    location: US
    versioning:
      enabled: true
    iamConfiguration:
      uniformBucketLevelAccess:
        enabled: true

- name: load-balancer
  type: compute.v1.forwardingRule
  properties:
    loadBalancingScheme: EXTERNAL
    target: $(ref.target-pool.selfLink)
    portRange: 80

- name: target-pool
  type: compute.v1.targetPool
  properties:
    region: us-central1
    instances:
    - $(ref.compute-instance.selfLink)

- name: firewall-rule
  type: compute.v1.firewall
  properties:
    network: global/networks/default
    sourceRanges: ['0.0.0.0/0']
    allowed:
    - IPProtocol: tcp
      ports: ['80']
    direction: INGRESS
    priority: 1000
